hero.heading: I help organizations build lasting structure in their systems.
hero.subtitle: Dependencies no one can fully trace anymore. Connections that have been lost. Decisions anchored nowhere. I work through it — until the root cause is visible and the structure holds.
observations.heading: What I observe
observations.1: Key person dependencies: knowledge and decisions that rest with individuals — and walk out the door when they leave.
observations.2: Shadow IT: teams adopting their own tools and processes — without a shared standard.
observations.3: Governance and guidelines that exist on paper — but never take hold, because they rely on discipline rather than structure.
observations.4: Systems that keep getting added to rather than properly structured. Even though changes have long been a risk.
observations.5: Software architectures, systems, and tooling that have outgrown everyone's understanding — where dependencies and connections are no longer fully visible, and every addition makes things more opaque.
principles.heading: Five principles
principles.1.title: Curiosity
principles.1.description: I approach every system without assumptions and examine what is actually there.
principles.2.title: Understanding
principles.2.description: I dig into the existing structures, dependencies, and connections.
principles.3.title: Reflection
principles.3.description: I validate my findings from multiple directions until the full picture holds.
principles.4.title: Foresight
principles.4.description: I assess what the system needs to deliver in the future — and what it takes to get there.
principles.5.title: Essence
principles.5.description: I reduce to what truly matters.
services.heading: How I support you
services.diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
services.diagnosis.subtitle: Straight to the root cause
services.diagnosis.description: You want certainty about what is really going on in your systems and tooling. I work through it layer by layer until the full picture is clear.
services.diagnosis.deliverable: Concrete evidence from your system. No guesswork.
services.guidance.title: Guidance
services.guidance.subtitle: From root cause to solution
services.guidance.description: The root cause is clear. Now you want a solution that is built to hold — and someone who guides its implementation.
services.guidance.deliverable: What I build is built to last — beyond the project, beyond individual people and technologies.
services.sparring.title: Sparring
services.sparring.subtitle: Alignment that grows with your system
services.sparring.description: Your system evolves, and with it the decisions that shape its future. You want someone who knows the full context and sees the big picture.
services.sparring.deliverable: Every decision is evaluated in the context of your entire system — not in isolation.
casestudy.heading: Case Studies

process.2.title: Approach
process.3.title: Scope

process.5.title: Result
about.heading: Who I am
about.text.1: Philipp Höllinger from Salzburg. I anchor decisions in structure — across software, tools, and organizations. I have an eye for the things that go unnoticed. The details that shape the future of a system.
about.text.2: My career began in logistics — coordinating processes between goods, people, and procedures. From there, I moved into software development and finally into architecture: automotive industry, public sector, software consulting.
about.text.3: What I observed along the way: the more people, tools, and AI come into play, the more important it becomes that decisions once made actually last.
about.text.4: I establish the structure that ensures exactly that.
about.text.5: That is why I founded Curfee.
calltoaction.heading: Let's talk about what's on your mind
about.who.heading: Philipp Höllinger
about.who.1: I anchor decisions in structure — across software, tools, and organizations. My career began in logistics — coordinating processes between goods, people, and procedures. From there, I moved into software development and finally into architecture: automotive industry, public sector, software consulting.
about.who.2: What I observed: the more tools, processes, and people interact with each other, the more important it becomes that decisions last and are anchored in structure — not in individual minds, not enforced through discipline — especially as teams grow, people change, and requirements shift.
about.who.3: Structure always exists. The question is whether it was intentionally designed — or simply emerged because no one was responsible for it. Responsibility often doesn't arise from a decision, but by accident — someone grows into a role without truly being in charge, and often without the decision-making authority or the expertise it requires. The role that would need to be accountable for structural quality doesn't exist in most projects — and its absence goes unnoticed until the consequences surface.
about.who.4: What drives me: an eye for the things that go unnoticed. The details that shape the future of a system.
about.done.heading: What I've done
about.done.example.1.heading: Justice
about.done.example.1.text: A booking system that didn't prevent errors — it enabled them. No transactional integrity, no validation rules, no database constraints. Rooms were double-booked, bookings in the past were possible, conflicting bookings went unchecked. The root cause wasn't poor development — it was the absence of structure that would have prevented these errors by design. I established it. Duplicate entries disappeared, performance improved by 20% — and for the first time, people could rely on a booked room actually being available for them.
about.done.example.2.heading: Public Administration
about.done.example.2.text: The EU Single Digital Gateway required administrative information to be available in a structured, multilingual, and machine-readable format — in German and English for EU citizens, crawlable for EU infrastructure. I designed a system that lets editors create and update content, tag it with metadata, and publish it automatically. A structured translation workflow was integrated — the first application of its kind in the region.
about.done.example.3.heading: Tourism & Leisure
about.done.example.3.text: For seven years, one student per region spent three months each summer reading emails and manually entering events. Five regions, five students, every year. The root cause wasn't a staffing problem. There was no standardized input format, no shared foundation. I designed a form that event organizers fill out themselves, an automated import that loads the data daily, and an approval mechanism for review and editing. What used to be a summer job is now a click.
about.stand.heading: What I stand for
about.stand.1.title: Root cause over symptom
about.stand.1.description: Real solutions start with understanding what is actually going on.
about.stand.2.title: Structure over discipline
about.stand.2.description: What holds must be embedded in the system, not in individual behavior.
about.stand.3.title: Lasting over workaround
about.stand.3.description: What I build outlasts people and technologies.
about.work.heading: How I work
about.work.1: I don't deliver quick answers; I deliver the right one. I work through the system until the root cause is clear, and reduce everything to what matters.
about.vision.heading: What is possible
about.vision.1.title: Shared alignment
about.vision.1.description: People with different interests — aligned by a shared foundation.
about.vision.2.title: Seamless tool integration
about.vision.2.description: New tools and systems that integrate seamlessly into the existing landscape — because the structure defines the boundaries.
about.vision.3.title: Resilient foundation
about.vision.3.description: Requirements and priorities that shift — supported by a structure designed to handle it.
about.vision.4.title: Lasting decisions
about.vision.4.description: Decisions that outlast people and technologies — embedded in the structure.
about.vision.5.title: Compound improvements
about.vision.5.description: Improvements that build on the last. And strengthen the foundation for the next.
about.vision.6.title: Transparent traceability
about.vision.6.description: Dependencies and connections that are visible — and decisions that anyone can trace, because they are anchored in the structure.
services.diagnosis.introduction: You have something specific on your mind and want certainty. I work through your system until the full picture is clear. After that, you have what you need — grounded in concrete evidence from your system.
services.guidance.introduction: The root cause is known, or we find it together. I design the solution and guide the implementation. The implementation itself is done by those with the expertise in the relevant systems, tools, or processes. I make sure the structure holds.
services.sparring.introduction: Your system evolves, and with it the decisions that shape its future. You want someone who knows your system and understands the context. I evaluate decisions, provide honest feedback, and make sure the structural direction holds.
faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Your question isn't here? Let's clarify it in the initial conversation.
faq.1.question: How does the first conversation work?
faq.1.answer: No pressure. You tell me what's on your mind, I listen. After that, we both know whether I can help and how.
faq.2.question: Do you implement yourself?
faq.2.answer: No. I design the solution and guide your team through the execution. The implementation is done by those who have the expertise in the relevant systems, tools, or processes.
faq.3.question: What happens after the diagnosis?
faq.3.answer: You decide. The Diagnosis stands on its own — you can take it from there. If you want, I can take it further with Guidance.
faq.4.question: Do you work remotely or on-site?
faq.4.answer: Both — depends on the scenario. Most of the work happens remotely. On-site when the situation requires it.
faq.5.question: Can I combine services?
faq.5.answer: Yes. A typical path starts with Diagnosis, continues into Guidance, and evolves into Sparring. Each service stands on its own, but they build on each other naturally.
faq.6.question: What exactly do you embed into the structure?
faq.6.answer: Standards, guidelines, and conventions — embedded directly into the structure across software, tools, and organizations, so they apply to everyone and everything automatically, without relying on individual discipline. They hold regardless of who comes and goes.
faq.7.question: How long does a typical engagement take?
faq.7.answer: It depends on the scope and complexity of the systems involved. We define it together in the first conversation.
faq.8.question: Do you work within agile frameworks?
faq.8.answer: No. My work requires concentrated, uninterrupted analysis and space for deep thinking. I integrate with your team's rhythm, but the analysis itself needs focused depth.
faq.9.question: Who is this relevant for?
faq.9.answer: Anyone working with software architectures, system and tool landscapes, or processes that have grown organically — and who wants clarity on dependencies, connections, and how decisions are anchored.
consent.message: This website uses cookies for usage analysis.
cookies.heading: Cookie Policy
cookies.what.heading: What are cookies
cookies.what: Cookies are small text files that are stored on the user's device when visiting a website. They serve to make websites more user-friendly and efficient. Cookies fall into two categories: first-party cookies, which are set by the website being visited, and third-party cookies, which are set by external services integrated into the website.
cookies.legal.heading: Legal basis
cookies.legal: The use of cookies is governed by §165(3) of the Austrian Telecommunications Act 2021 (TKG 2021), which implements Art. 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive. Technically necessary cookies are set on the basis of the legitimate interest of the data controller pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR, as they are required for the proper operation of the website. Non-essential cookies are only set with the explicit consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.
cookies.types.heading: Types of cookies used
cookies.types.necessary.label: Necessary cookies
cookies.types.necessary: These cookies are required for the basic functionality and security of the website. They are set without consent. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
cookies.types.analytics.label: Analytics cookies (Google Analytics)
cookies.types.analytics: These cookies are used to measure website usage and to improve the website based on usage data. They are only set with the explicit consent of the user. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Provider: Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Retention period: 14 months. Further details are provided in the privacy policy.
cookies.list.heading: Cookie list
cookies.list.name: Name
cookies.list.gastar.name: _ga_*
cookies.manage.heading: Managing cookies
cookies.manage: Cookies can be managed and deleted at any time via the browser settings. Consent to non-essential cookies can be withdrawn at any time. Instructions for managing cookies can be found in the help sections of your browser.
cookies.updates.heading: Updates to this policy
cookies.updates: This cookie policy may be updated to reflect changes in the cookies used or for legal reasons. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page.
privacy.cookies.reference: Details on the cookies used on this website are provided in the cookie policy.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.phone.label: Phone
privacy.scope: This privacy policy informs users of this website, in accordance with Art. 13 of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), about the nature, scope, and purpose of the processing of personal data by the data controller.
privacy.controller.heading: Data controller
privacy.personal.heading: Processing of personal data
privacy.personal: Personal data is collected on this website only to the extent that is technically necessary. Processing is subject to purpose limitation and data minimisation. Collected data is under no circumstances disclosed to third parties without a legal basis. The processing of personal data is based on the following legal grounds: Consent pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR — the data subject has given consent to the processing of their personal data for one or more specific purposes. Performance of a contract pursuant to Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party, or in order to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract. Legitimate interests pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party, except where such interests are overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject.
privacy.rights.heading: Rights of data subjects
privacy.rights: As a data subject, you have the following rights: Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR) — You have the right to obtain confirmation as to whether personal data is being processed, as well as information about such data and its processing. Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR) — You have the right to request the rectification of inaccurate personal data. Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR) — You have the right to request the erasure of your personal data, provided the legal requirements are met. Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR) — You have the right to request the restriction of processing of your personal data. Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR) — You have the right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format. Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR) — You have the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to your particular situation, to the processing of personal data concerning you.
privacy.withdrawal.heading: Right of withdrawal
privacy.withdrawal: Pursuant to Art. 7(3) GDPR, you have the right to withdraw consent at any time. The lawfulness of processing carried out on the basis of consent before its withdrawal is not affected. Withdrawal can be declared by email.
privacy.complaint.heading: Right to lodge a complaint
privacy.complaint: If you believe that the processing of your personal data violates the GDPR, you have the right, pursuant to Art. 77 GDPR, to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority.
privacy.ssl.heading: SSL/TLS encryption
privacy.ssl: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of personal data, this website uses SSL or TLS encryption. An encrypted connection can be identified by the padlock icon in the address bar and by the address beginning with "https://".
privacy.cookies.heading: Cookies
privacy.links.heading: Links to third-party websites
privacy.links: This website contains links to third-party websites. When accessing these external links, data may be transmitted to the respective operator. The data controller assumes no liability for the content and data processing by third parties. Users are encouraged to review the privacy policies of linked websites.
privacy.hosting.heading: Hosting
privacy.hosting: This website is hosted on GitHub Pages, a service provided by GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P Kelly Jr St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. When accessing this website, information is automatically recorded in so-called server log files, which the browser transmits automatically. This includes: IP address, date and time of access, requested resources, browser and operating system used. This data is stored temporarily for security purposes and deleted after a short period. Processing is based on the legitimate interest pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR to ensure the uninterrupted operation of the website. For the transfer of personal data to the USA (third country), GitHub employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR. GitHub is also certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.heading: Google Analytics
privacy.analytics: This website uses Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Google Analytics is activated exclusively with the consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Consent preferences are stored for 30 days. The following data is collected: pages visited and timestamps, interactions on the website, traffic source, device and browser information, geographic information (country/region), IP address (anonymized). Data is retained for 14 months. Google employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR and is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.more: Further information on how Google Analytics handles user data can be found in Google's privacy policy.

services.ideal.heading: Your situation
services.differentiator.heading: The standard
services.diagnosis.ideal.1: You are working with a systems and tooling landscape that has grown organically — and you no longer know where the real risks and dependencies lie.
services.diagnosis.ideal.2: Any change — to tools, processes, or systems — has unforeseen consequences, because dependencies and connections cannot be traced.
services.diagnosis.ideal.3: You want confidence before making decisions — based on concrete evidence, not gut feeling.
services.diagnosis.differentiator: Each insight points to a precise location within your system. The path from symptom to root cause is traceable step by step and can be independently verified. The results are ready to act on.
services.guidance.ideal.1: The root cause is known, or you want to find it together and resolve it directly.
services.guidance.ideal.2: Guidelines and standards exist — but are not followed, because there is no structure to anchor them.
services.guidance.ideal.3: You want solutions that survive the next staff turnover, tool migration, or reorganization — not just a quick fix that treats the symptom.
services.guidance.differentiator: Standards, conventions, and guidelines embedded in the structure that apply to every person and every tool.
services.sparring.ideal.1: Your systems are evolving — and with them the decisions that determine whether dependencies and connections remain manageable.
services.sparring.ideal.2: Teams and departments are building their own tooling and workflows. You want someone who ensures the shared direction holds — before the system landscape diverges.
services.sparring.ideal.3: You value well-founded feedback that considers the entire system.
services.sparring.differentiator: My feedback always considers the full context of your system — not just the decision at hand, but how it aligns with the standards, conventions, and guidelines embedded in the structure.
